result.summary: Discord exposes a developer HTTP API at https://discord.com/api, a real-time Gateway at wss://gateway.discord.gg, and public incoming webhook endpoints; authentication uses bot tokens or Discord OAuth2 application credentials.
result.description: Discord is a communications platform for communities, apps, and games. Its developer platform lets developers build bots, companion apps, activities, and game/social integrations that interact with Discord users, servers, and messages.

discord_bot_token.label: Discord bot token
discord_bot_token.setup: In the [Discord Developer Portal](https://discord.com/developers/applications), create an application, open its **Bot** settings, and add a bot user. Discord generates the bot token there; this is the token your code uses to call the API and identify on Gateway. The docs note that your bot token, OAuth2 credentials, and configuration live in the Developer Portal.

discord_oauth_app.label: Discord OAuth2 application credentials
discord_oauth_app.setup: Register an application in the [Discord Developer Portal](https://discord.com/developers/applications) to get a `client_id` and `client_secret`. Use those for Discord OAuth2 flows such as authorization code and client credentials via `https://discord.com/oauth2/authorize` and `https://discord.com/api/oauth2/token`. Choose the scopes your integration needs in the app settings and authorization URL.

surfaces.name: Discord HTTP API
surfaces.notes: Discord documents both bot-based and OAuth2-based access to the same REST API. Some webhook execution endpoints are tokenized and the webhook resource docs say incoming webhooks do not require a bot user or authentication to use, but these are endpoints within the same HTTP API rather than a separate API surface.
